name: Veronica Ann Bennet
description_source_text:
Veronica Ann Bennet is a Gazorpian who lives in the underground city of Paradise on the planet Gazorpazorp. She was put on trial for having bad bangs and was sentenced to "the silent treatment".

Veronica was arrested because her bangs were not up to standard, or just because they were not cut right. She was placed in line before the Gazorpian judge in order to be handed her punishment. While standing in line in front of Rick Sanchez and Summer Smith, she had a short chat with Rick on why he was on trial. Before she could say what she was there for, she was called before the judge who read up her charges. The judge sentenced her to "the silent treatment", effectively having the rest of the gazorpian women ignore her, including her friend Jackie.

name: Voltematron
description_source_text:
is a parasitic alien which once possessed Morty Smith. It appeared in the episode "Morty's Mind Blowers".

Voltamatron, somehow, entered Morty's body at some point prior to the episode "Morty's Mind Blowers". Presumably in order to destroy Earth, it possessed Morty in front of the latter's family and showcased its cosmic powers by levitating objects around him. Rick knew the only way to overpower Voltamatron was to show Morty love and urged the Smiths to do so. Telling Morty they love him, Voltamatron was slowly forced out of Morty; however, the Smiths quickly lost their concentration and started complaining about Morty instead. This allowed Voltamatron to start re-entering Morty, causing him great pain. This incident was later removed from Morty's memory and placed in Rick's memory chamber.

name: Wall Crawling Rick
description_source_text:
Lizard Rick is a lizard person-version of Rick seen climbing walls at The Citadel.

He first appeared in the Season 3 premiere episode, "The Rickshank Rickdemption", and can be spotted crawling on the walls of the Citadel. It is unknown what happened to him following the fall of the Galactic Federation and near destruction of The Citadel.

He later makes a cameo in "Rickmurai Jack" in Rick C-137's flashback, where he appears on the wall with many photos of many Ricks belonging to the future Council alliance, including himself. Rick C-137 later went on a rampage killing those Ricks, but considering he remained alive many years later, Rick probably lost him in his blind rage.

name: Wedding Bartender
description_source_text:
The wedding bartender is a moopydoop who worked as a bartender on Planet Squanch for a wedding. Whether he was a casualty at the wedding or left beforehand is unknown.

He appeared as the main bartender for the wedding of Tammy Gueterman and Birdperson. He was serving Rick drinks and made a comment that he "had a lot of vodka" when Rick complained about Morty giving him bad advice.

His fate after the wedding is unknown as Tammy revealed that the whole wedding was a trap and he was never seen in the massacre that followed.

They are three unknown alternate dimension creatures that made appearances on that dimensions' version of Saturday Night Live. The announcer could not pronounce their name, so basically all information about them is completely unknown.

name: Woman Rick
description_source_text: Woman Rick is an inanimate Rick character who appeared in the episode Close Rick-counters of the Rick Kind. She appeared acting as one of the chairs in the fake dimensions. She is in Rick's clothing when Rick and Morty run from the Council of Ricks.

name: Worldender
description_source_text:
Worldender is a supervillain and an antagonist who appeared in "Vindicators 3: The Return of Worldender". He is the arch-nemesis of The Vindicators. His villainy, as his name implies, is revealed to be destroying planets and committing genocide against numerous alien species.

Little is known about Worldender, other than the fact that he revels in the genocide of entire species and destroying worlds. Whether he does this to accomplish some homicidal goal, feeds on the worlds he destroys, or does it simply for his enjoyment is unknown. He has suffered defeat at the hands of The Vindicators in the past, though how many times is unknown.

In "Vindicators 3: The Return of Worldender", The Vindicators assemble to thwart Worldender once again, only to find that Rick Sanchez had slaughtered Worldender and his forces the night before while on a drunken bender, leaving Worldender's barely-alive body on display, disemboweled and suspended from the ceiling by a meat hook.

His corpse would appear later in the episode, being used as photo prop at Rick's party.

Rick's backstory of Rickmurai Jack shows what seems to be Yellow Shirt Rick getting killed by Rick C-137.

name: Zeep Xanflorp
description_source_text:
Zeep Xanflorp is a scientist within Rick's Microverse Battery who creates his own micro-planet power supply called the Miniverse. His development of a new power supply prevents Rick from using his flying car. Zeep is depicted as arrogant and very intelligent, and in many ways a parallel of Rick Sanchez.

Zeep appears to function as the Microverse's version of Rick Sanchez. Much like the latter, Zeep is arrogant, self-loathing and prideful; traits which are outshined by his sheer genius. Zeep constantly talks down to the people around him and shows little care for social status when doing so. He is condescending towards Chris, the president of his home planet, and refuses to make time for Rick and Morty, despite Rick being a revered figure in his planet's culture.

Zeep also mimicks Rick's ruthless personality. Once he, Rick and Morty were trapped inside the Teenyverse, Zeep constantly tried to one-up Rick and tried to kill him multiple times. He also betrayed Rick after the two built a way to escape the Teenyverse and attempted to destroy the Miniverse Battery with Rick and Morty inside it. This, of course, would've also destroyed an entire universe of his own creation, fully demonstrating his ruthless capabilities. However, after Rick destroyed the Miniverse Battery instead, Zeep was temporarily distraught and called Rick a "monster". This may imply Zeep never intended to destroy the battery and was merely bluffing.

Despite his similarities to Rick, Zeep is seemingly more altrusitic than him. Whilst Rick invented the Microverse Battery just to power his car or charge his phone, Zeep invented his Miniverse Battery to benefit his entire society.

name: Zeta Alpha Rick
description_source_text:
Zeta Alpha Rick was a member of the now defunct Council of Ricks. He was killed by Rick C-137 in "The Rickshank Rickdemption."

In "Close Rick-Counters of the Rick Kind", Zeta Alpha Rick appears alongside his fellow council-members, Rick Prime, Maximums Rickimus, Quantum Rick, Riq IV, and Ricktiminus Sancheziminius, accusing Rick C-137 for the murder of 27 alternate Ricks, including The Scientist Formerly Known as Rick. He joined them in sentencing Rick C-137 to the machine of unspeakable doom, to which he steals a portal gun and escapes from the Citadel.

After the real perpetrator, Evil Rick, was beaten to death by the group of enslaved Mortys, the Council of Ricks shows up to escort the Rick-less Mortys back to their respective dimensions. The Council takes C-137 Rick and Morty back to the Citadel, and officially apologize to Rick for accusing him of the murders. They even offer him a coupon for a free Morty, should anything ever happen to his, which Rick accepts while Morty's back is turned.

 .]]
In "The Rickshank Rickdemption", the Council of Ricks hold Morty and Summer captive after sending SEAL Team Ricks to assassinate Rick C-137. After Rick teleported the entire Citadel back into the middle of the Federation prison, a heated battle broke out between the two sides. Rick infiltrated the Council chamber and kills most of the Council of Ricks, killing Zeta Alpha Rick with a shot to the head before ending up in a standoff with Riq IV, ultimately killing him and wiping out the council.

name: Zick Zack
description_source_text:
Zick Zack is a Floop Floopian warlord who sought to be killed by a great warrior, Rick Sanchez. He was later hit by a car and his spirit was dragged down under the ground by demonic spirits.

Zick Zack teleported to the Dimension C-131 and sought out Rick Sanchez, whom he considered a great warrior. Rick was first surprised to be visited by an alien overlord, before Zick Zack handed him a paper explaining who Zick Zack was and what he wanted; to achieve an eternal orgasmic afterlife by getting killed by a great warrior. Rick was flattered at first, but took Zick Zack to lunch first.

 When Rick went to the bathroom, Zick Zack started to doubt his promised afterlife after a conversation with Morty. No longer wishing to die, Zick Zack ran out of the restaurant to escape from Rick, but was hit by a car in the middle of the road. His spirit emerged from his body and was immediately grabbed by demonic spirits, confirming the existence of a Floop Floopian afterlife.

Due to not being killed by a great warrior, Zick Zack's spirit was dragged underground by the demons. As he wails in agony, Zick Zack blames Morty for causing him to be condemned to a terrible fate.

name: Uncle Steve
description_source_text:
Uncle Steve was a minor character that appeared in "Total Rickall". He was a Memory Parasite who had taken on the form of Steve, Jerry Smith's older brother.

He claimed to be the eldest son of Leonard Smith and Joyce Smith, the nephew of the Unnamed Uncle, the older brother of Jerry Smith, the brother-in-law of Beth Smith, and the paternal uncle of Summer Smith and Morty Smith. He had only been in the house a short while, but implanted memories in the Smith Family's minds to convince them they had known Steve their entire lives and he had been living with them for nearly a year. Morty also thought Steve taught him how to ride a bike in his youth.

During breakfast, Jerry discovered Steve had booked the Smith Family tickets to Paris. This was apparently to thank the Smiths for letting him stay with them, but was more likely an attempt for the Memory Parasites to spread across the globe. Rick entered the room and promptly asked who Steve was. Jerry "reminded" Rick of Steve's presence, allowing Rick to deduce he was a Memory Parasite. Rick then executed Steve and debunked all of Steve's claims made to the family.

Uncle Steve was shown to be incredibly kind, supportive and encouraging in his brief appearance. This is likely a biproduct of the Memory Parasites only being able to implant good memories in their victims' minds. He reassured his "brother", Jerry, that he would find a job soon and any employer would be lucky to have him. Steve also attempted to make fun of Rick not knowing who he was by teasing him, although this did little to prevent Rick from killing him. Despite this front, his true nature of a Memory Parasite was hinted at by booking tickets for the Smith Family to fly to Paris, which was seemingly a ploy to help spread his species across the world.

name: Roy
description_source_text:
Roy Parsons is the eponymous character of the virtual reality arcade game "Roy: A Life Well Lived" which is played by both Morty and Rick at the intergalactic arcade Blips and Chitz in the episode "Mortynight Run".

Roy's personality depends on whoever is playing the game. Regardless, most people forget about their real life and believe themselves to be Roy as long as Roy remains alive. Some people, such as Rick Sanchez, are able to remain conscious both inside and outside the game at the same time, effectively giving Roy their own personalities. This is most likely due to Rick's status as a megagenius, implying highly intelligent individuals are able to remember their real life as they play.

Roy's biography is not predetermined, as he is a video game character and his story is entirely dependent on the player's decisions.

Roy Parsons (C-131)

However, when Morty played as Roy in the episode "Mortynight Run," Roy first appeared as a child who had a nightmare. His mother reassured him, and he went to school. While there, Roy looked out the window during class and saw other kids playing American football. This inspired him to take up the sport and later join the school team. At one of the matches, he caught the eye of a girl in the stands named Rosy. They fell in love immediately and later married, having an unnamed son. However, a few years later, they ran into financial problems. Rosy told Roy he needed to be more realistic and talk to her father about the carpet store. He later developed cancer, and Rosy supported him throughout his treatment. After his recovery, she brought her wheelchair-bound husband home, where she and other family members celebrated his recovery. One workday, one of his colleagues asked him to move a Persian carpet. Roy climbed the ladder, but suddenly fell and died.

In the "Rick and Morty Presents: Mr. Meeseeks" comic, one of the Mr. Meeseekses decides to play as him. He wakes up and goes to help his mom make chili. A few years later, he began playing American football at the school. He later had a falling out with his father over football. There he met Rosy. They began spending time together and later began dating. She later accused Roy of cheating on her, and they broke up. He later dropped out of school and became effectively unemployed. He began to spend his time in bars, drinking heavily, out of grief. However, Roy found the strength to overcome his alcohol addiction and remained sober for at least a year. He unexpectedly ran into Rosy, and they decided to eat together at a restaurant. She was surprised he didn't order chili; he said people change. Rosy told him she thought love was her destiny. However, she later realized that love grows when two caring people share connection and understanding. Suddenly, a ventilation shaft fell from the ceiling onto her, and she died. Mr. Meeseeks, playing as Roy, was surprised by her death but was glad that death was possible in this game. He killed Roy by stabbing him, after which he played the game many times, dying in different ways.

Roy Parsons (Parmesan Dimension)

In the episode "Rick: A Mort Well Lived", Rick plays as the character in order to save Morty and get him out of the game after he is trapped in the game when his mind becomes splintered into all of the NPC characters in the game.

name: Davin
description_source_text:
Davin was Beth and Tom's coworker at St. Equis Hospital. Davin was in love with Beth and he constantly hit on her and tried to seduce her. Because of this, Jerry (his colleague Beth's husband) was extremely jealous of him, despite Beth denying having any feelings for him.

In the Pilot, Davin was seen working with his colleagues Beth and Tom on saving a horse's life. He later comes back and interrupts husband and wife's fight, telling Beth how they did a very beautiful and passionate thing together, saving that horse's life like that. It's obvious that Davin was hitting on her, and trying to swoon her over, with the way he was being so poetic with her. In response to this, Jerry slams the door shut and says "Since we're fighting, if you ever have an affair with that guy I will come to the hotel room and blow my brains out all over your naked bodies."

In "Rick Potion No. 9", Beth is called into work late at night by Davin and Tom to help him after a horse racing track had a starlight derby where there was a "seven-horse collision". After Beth and Davin save the horses Davin tries to seduce the woman. After sneezing, Davin becomes afflicted with the "Morty love potion" where he becomes obsessed with Morty (his colleague Beth's son). Beth then runs away from Davin when the man turns into one of the "Mantis-people" as a result of Rick's initial cure for the Morty love potion. Jerry bursts in the door and beats Davin to death with a crowbar, saving his wife's life.

In the Parmesan Dimension, Davin is seen in "The CuRicksous Case of Bethjamin Button" helping and assisting Beth in saving multiple horses' lives.

Davin is a very skilled and savvy doctor, who takes his job as a horse surgeon very seriously and executes his surgeries with unmolested confidence. He's a rather macho guy, who makes less-than-subtle passes at Beth, who he attains a palpable crush on. His plays range from hitting on her to shamelessly enclosing her in a candle-lit room with him, despite knowing full well that she's married, which kind of makes him a bit of a douche.

description_source_text:
Giant Court is a court that Rick and Morty are taken to in the episode Meeseeks and Destroy after they "kill" Dale, and a few minutes later his wife along with their son catches them in the act. name: Morty Jr's interviewer
description_source_text:
:For the version in Pocket Mortys, see Morty Jr. (Pocket Mortys). For the version from the episode Morty Daddy, see Morty Jr. (Parmesan Dimension).

Mortimer "Morty" Smith Jr. from Dimension C-131 is Morty Smith's son. He was conceived by Morty and a Gazorpian sex robot named Gwendolyn in the episode "Raising Gazorpazorp." Morty Jr. grew from an infant to a full adult at a hyper-increased rate, allowing him to quickly outgrow his father.

Being half-Gazorpian, Morty Jr. is prone to violence and destruction and has a difficult time fending off these urges. While he has the human tendencies to listen and be non-violent, his Gazorpian genetics influence him to seek out destruction and death. After being swayed with words, he decides to be a creative type and later channels his destructive nature in writing.

Post-credits, an aged Morty Jr. has an interview about a book he wrote, "My Horrible Father". Becoming much calmer and suppressing his Gazorpian urges to kill and conquer, Morty Jr. expresses a cynic and dark view of his childhood, criticizing his father Morty for raising him imprisoned, even though he knew of his own violent and sadistic senses and would have most likely killed many innocent people if he had not changed.

description_source_text:
The Reverse Height Universe appears in Rixty Minutes and features a universe that has the swapped heights of tall and short people where it is normal to be "short" and abnormal to be "tall".

This is demonstrated by an alternate version of Game of Thrones where everyone is short, but Tyrion Lannister is actually normal-sized.

name: Quick Mystery Presenter
description_source_text:
Quick Mystery is an interdimensional TV show hosted by a grown man in a trenchcoat, most likely a crime detective. He introduces each quick mystery by saying a variation of "Here's a quick mystery".

Rick randomly switches to this show in "Rixty Minutes" and the Smith family watches it for a short while before continuing to flip through channels.

Each episode consists of clips of short murder mysteries where a murder has been committed and the perpetrator stands forward and admits to the murder while also presenting the murder weapon. That person is then arrested and convicted by a judge to jail or execution.

Quick Mystery is a parody of the long running documentary television series Unsolved Mysteries, which was originally hosted by actor Robert Stack from 1987 to 2002. On the show Stack, often dressed in a trenchcoat, would introduce and narrate segments about unsolved crimes and mysterious supernatural events. Quick Mystery is also a satire of the detective genre by presenting mysteries in a simple, unrealistic and anti-climactic way. It mocks detective stories like Sherlock Holmes, Poirot and Miss Marple by showing what they would be like if all of the complexities were removed. This is one of many detective genre satires within Rixty Minutes with another being Baby Legs.

name: Two Brothers
description_source_text:
Two Brothers, also known as Alien Invasion Tomato Monster Mexican Armada Brothers Who Are Just Regular Brothers Running In A Van From An Asteroid And All Sorts Of Things The Movie, is a movie produced by Glorpian Studios that exists in an alternate dimension. The movie's trailer was shown on "Rixty Minutes". In the trailer for the movie, two brothers are seen combating the obstacles of meteors, giant cat monsters, a tornando, a Mexican armada with tomato weapons, old gladiator women, and even the moon colliding with the planet. The two brothers can be seen at the end of the trailer appearing overwhelmed by the ridiculousness.

Jan Quadrant Vincent 16, a movie trailer animated and announced in the same exact style as Two Brothers was introduced in "Interdimensional Cable 2: Tempting Fate".

name: Giant Cat Monster
description_source_text:
Cat Rick Sanchez is an alternative version of Rick from a dimension where humans are cat-people, he appeared briefly in the background in Rick and Morty Issue 22, he is the father of Cat Beth, and the maternal grandfather of Cat Morty. He is a member of the Citadel's militia.

Cat Rick was sent with a group of other Ricks by the Council of Ricks to apprehend Doofus Jerry after he beat up Rick Sanchez. This caused Doofus Jerry to wonder about how many other dimensions before Cat Rick and another Rick hauled him through their portal.

At the Citadel of Ricks, Cat Rick found it amusing when another Rick told them about the last time a Jerry walked into the Genetic Restructurer and came out with two butts. This amusement was short lasted when Doofus Jerry exited the Genetic Restructurer and had altered his pheromones to be particularly poisonous to Ricks, killing a lot of the Ricks in the courtroom, Cat Rick fortunately survives, and returns to home with his daughter and grandson.

description_source_text:
Old Lady Thompson was a neighbour of the Smith Family back before Snuffles moved to Planet Dogg-One.

She is an elderly woman who needs a walker to move around, she seems to have a temper and she has a deep hatred for the dogs. She frightened Jerry on several occasions when loudly complaining about his dog defecating on her lawn. In these situations, Jerry would hide with Morty from her.

She was brought up in Rick and Morty Issue 21 by Morty as an example of how cowardly his father is, as he kept hiding from her instead of facing her like an adult.

name: Trunkphobic guy
description_source_text:
Diablo Verde was a Mexican superhero and a member of The Vindicators who was mentioned in "Vindicators 3: The Return of Worldender". He was never seen, but he was mentioned to have died, along with Lady Katana and Calypso, in battle with Doom-Nomitron.

In Vindicators 2: "Girls Night" (a spin-off show published to Adult Swim's YouTube channel), it is revealed that she never fought Doom-Nomitron, and was actually killed by Supernova when she blacked out drunk. Although she is voiced by a male voice actor and has masculine features, their gender is up to debate, as he is considered to be a member of the “girls”, and having feminine gestures from time to time.

While not much is known, Diablo Verde can be assumed as a heroic and kind person, joining the Vindicators to do good. She is also somewhat polite, congratulating Supernova while admonishing said heroine for her ridiculous plan.

She also had a good relationship with Lady Katana and Calypso, since Supernova mentioned that she, Lady Katana and Calypso had "inside jokes" with each other, in Vindicators 2 episode "Girls Night"

name: Baby Legs Chief
description_source_text:
Baby Legs is a character who appeared in "Rixty Minutes".

Baby Legs is a detective on a murder mystery TV series in an alternate dimension that features him and a partner named Regular Legs. Baby Legs is a full-grown adult with the legs of a baby. He has a high-pitched, nasally voice.

In the episode shown, Baby Legs is said to be a good detective, but not good enough because of his baby legs. He is partnered up with Regular Legs, which Baby Legs is not happy about. The two of them are sent out after a criminal which baby Legs tracks down to a warehouse. When the criminal runs, Baby Legs tries to chase him, but is slow and falls over due to his baby legs. He says he learned a valuable lesson and Regular Legs chases down the criminal. They both receive praise from their Police Captain before he tells them both to "get the f**k out of here".

Baby Legs is shown to be a good police detective, tracking a murderer down to a warehouse when Regular Legs couldn't, and is quite aware of how good he is. He used to be stubborn on that he doesn't need a partner with regular legs to make up for his baby legs, but learns when chasing the murderer that his baby legs do, in fact, limit him. He accepts Regular Legs as his partner after learning that lesson.

name: Plutonian Host
description_source_text:
Scroopy Noopers is a scientist and outcast on the celestial dwarf Pluto. He first appeared in Something Ricked This Way Comes.

Before meeting Morty, Scroopy Noopers was protesting outside of Pluto's Ministry of Money's fundraising, yelling about how the corporations are shrinking Pluto by mining out the plutonium in the planet's core.

Scroopy finds Morty at the set of Good Morning Pluto where Jerry is a guest. After introducing himself he brings Morty, at gunpoint, to a plutonium mine to show him how Pluto shrinks from the mining. He asks Morty to tell Jerry to stop saying that Pluto is a planet, which goes unsuccessfully.

Later, he is captured by the plutonian police and brought before his father, King Flippy Nips, who sends him to Plutanamo Bay military prison. The revelation that Flippy Nips would have his own son arrested for telling the truth makes Jerry realize that Morty is more important than what he believes is true leading him to tell the Plutonians about the true status of their planet.

ru:Скрупи Нуперз

He is an environmentalist who realizes that the Plutonian leaders are using Jerry to convince the citizens that their planet is not in danger. Although he is the son of the king, he is a rebel and an outcast who is not afraid of going against the strong members of society. Noopers is presumably in Plutanamo Bay serving a prison sentence for treason.

Rick later brought Morty with him and lied that he’s “a boy with space cancer who wants to see deader people in order to feel superior”. Michael believes it and grants them access to the morgue.

name: Veterinary
description_source_text:
(née Sanchez) is an alternate version of the original Beth, who appeared in the episode Close Rick-Counters of the Rick Kind, she is the daughter of Rick Sanchez and Mrs. Sanchez, the wife of Jerry Smith, and the mother of Summer Smith and Morty Smith, she is also the daughter-in-law of Leonard Smith and Joyce Smith, and the granddaughter of Rick's Father and a unnamed woman.

In the episode Close Rick-Counters of the Rick Kind, Beth and her family were sitting around the table, eating breakfast. Beth was telling her father Rick that it was the one year anniversary of Rick returning to the house, after being gone from her life for so long. She suggested that she makes flying saucer shaped pancakes to celebrate, but he declined to it. Just then, Evil Rick and Evil Morty opened a portal, walked into the room, and shot her father Rick as well as tranquilizing her son Morty and taking the boy with them, leaving Beth screaming at the top of her lungs in terror, along with her husband and daughter.

Beth is an intelligent, ambitious and independent woman who suffers from a variety of deep-seated insecurities. A dedicated veterinary horse surgeon, she is the family's main breadwinner and often tries to do the best for her family based on this sense of responsibility. She is insecure about her profession, however, and is quick to snap at the implication she is not a 'real' surgeon or a 'real' veterinarian, since she generally is a horse surgeon.

name: Veterinary Nurse
description_source_text: "A Rickle in Time" is the first episode of the second season of Rick and Morty. It is the twelfth episode of the series overall. It premiered on July 26, 2015. It was written by Matt Roller and directed by Wes Archer. The episode is rated TV-14-L.

name: Bearded Jerry
description_source_text:
Jerry Smith, of the Grand Central Dimension, also known as Boss Jerry, is the owner of Grand Central, a stopping point within the multiverse where Nomad Jerrys could pass through for a price. He served as the main antagonist in "Nomortland".

Boss Jerry lived in a version of the Smith Residence that notably lacked any of the other Smith Family members. It is implied Boss Jerry's family went on an adventure and never came back, leaving him alone. He formed a gang of Jerrys whose families hadn't returned from their adventures and subsequently turned the house into Grand Central, an interdimensional station where Jerrys travelling the multiverse could travel through certain routes by paying Boss Jerry. He also kept a druggie version of Rick as his slave and began wearing a leather jacket, which according to this Rick, turned Boss Jerry into a much meaner person.

Boss Jerry is first introduced when Mooch Jerry takes Jerry Smith of Dimension 5126 to Grand Central so they can request to get on the "Tundra Line" in order to get Jerry 5126 home. After it is revealed Boss Jerry employs Jerrys to steal from their multiversal counterparts in order to pay him. The meeting quickly goes sour and Boss Jerry blacklists Mooch Jerry and Jerry 5126 from travelling through the cracks in the multiverse. However, the two are able to get back to Grand Central and a chase ensues. Boss Jerry pursues the duo with his Bandana Jerry guards through multiple dimensions. It eventually ends with Boss Jerry trying to kill Jerry 5126 as the two climb through a burlap sack portal, where Mooch Jerry cuts the line, causing the portal to collapse in on itself. Boss Jerry is last seen falling through a void, presumably the space between universes, which seems to kill him or sent to another dimension.

Boss Jerry was a far more powerful and threatening Jerry compared to most of his multiversal counterparts. Before his singular appearance, Boss Jerry had decided he did not need to rely on Rick Sanchez the way other Jerrys do; Ricks need Jerrys to rely on. This is demonstrated by his enslavement and general treatment of Druggie Rick, who was frequently physically abused by Boss Jerry and made to follow his orders. Despite this, Boss Jerry is still shown treating his fellow Jerrys with respect if they pay him the demanded fare for using Grand Central, although relations can quickly sour if they step out of line. Boss Jerry is also not afraid of killing if need be, as he attempted to shoot Jerry 5126 and Mooch Jerry when they attempted to escape him.

name: Shaved Head Jerry
description_source_text:
The Abandoned Jerrys are a group of Jerrys who reside in the Jerryboree because their Ricks and Mortys never returned to pick them up. They live in a backroom, away from the Jerrys who only stay at Jerryboree temporarily.

They received a new temporary member in Jerry Smith, with whom they played cards. He came up with an idea to escape the daycare, to which they stated that no one in Jerryboree is kept against their will. When Jerry asked them why they chose to remain in the daycare, they answered with the same reason he himself was still there; because "they're Jerrys", coupled with the fact that the world outside the building is comparatively unknown and potentially hostile.

The abandoned Jerrys will all have returned to their original universes after the events of "Solaricks".

name: Jerryboree Receptionist
description_source_text:
The unnamed Jerryboree receptionist is an alien who appears in the episodes "Mortynight Run" and "Bethic Twinstinct".

The Jerryboree receptionist is first seen in the episode "Mortynight Run" when Jerry is checked into the Jerryboree. She greets him, walks him to the back section, and leaves him with the other Jerrys. She is seen again when Jerry tells her he wants to leave, and she directs him to a tube which leads back inside via a slide. Later, he announces that he is leaving and she tells him that it was always allowed. She is once again seen when Jerry arrives back to the Jerryboree, and she watches him walk back in while smiling at him.

She is also seen in the episode "Bethic Twinstinct." She greets Jerry when he arrives at the Jerryboree to check himself in with the intention of having an affair with another Jerry. She informs him that he can't check himself in, due to past issues when Jerrys try to touch other Jerrys, at which point he leaves.

name: Roy's Wife
description_source_text:
Rosy Parsons, also known as Roy's wife, is the second eponymous character of the virtual reality arcade game "Roy: A Life Well Lived" which appears at the intergalactic arcade Blips and Chitz in the episode "Mortynight Run".

Rosy's personality depends on whoever is playing the game. Regardless, most people forget about their real life and believe themselves to be Rosy as long as Rosy remains alive. Some people, such as Rick Sanchez, are able to remain conscious both inside and outside the game at the same time, effectively giving Roy and Rosy their own personalities. This is most likely due to Rick's status as a megagenius, implying highly intelligent individuals are able to remember their real life as they play.

Rosy's biography is not predetermined, as she is a video game character and her story is entirely dependent on the player's decisions.

However, when Morty played as Roy Parsons in the episode "Mortynight Run," Rosy first appeared as a fan in the stands who immediately took a liking to Roy. They eventually married and had a son. A few years later, they ran into financial trouble. Rosy told Roy he needed to be more realistic and talk to her father about the carpet store. He later developed cancer, and Rosy supported him throughout his treatment. After his recovery, she brought her wheelchair-bound husband home, where she and other family members celebrated his recovery.

In the "Rick and Morty Presents: Mr. Meeseeks" comic, one of the Mr. Meeseekses decides to play as her. She wakes up and says she needs to find a boy and have a relationship with him before they die. Her mother supported her in this. However, nothing worked out for her. Her mother told her that love was dead and cited Rosy's father as an example, who cheated on her by starting a relationship with his intern. A few years later, her friends told her about Roy and that she should meet him. They later did so and began a relationship. However, after some time, she suspected him of cheating and left him. Some time later, her father died. Rosy graduated from university and got a job in a carpet store. She believed it was what she was meant to do with her life. After a while, she got an office job. A colleague approached her and bombarded her with tedious paperwork, explaining that Rosy was the only one home with no one waiting. Unexpectedly, she and Roy ran into each other again and decided to eat and discuss their lives at a restaurant. She was surprised he didn't order chili; he said people change. Rosy told him she thought love was her destiny. However, she later realized that love grows when two caring people share connection and understanding. Suddenly, a ventilation shaft fell from the ceiling onto her, and she died.

name: Roy's Son
description_source_text:
Roy Parsons is the eponymous character of the virtual reality arcade game "Roy: A Life Well Lived" which is played by both Morty and Rick at the intergalactic arcade Blips and Chitz in the episode "Mortynight Run".

Roy's personality depends on whoever is playing the game. Regardless, most people forget about their real life and believe themselves to be Roy as long as Roy remains alive. Some people, such as Rick Sanchez, are able to remain conscious both inside and outside the game at the same time, effectively giving Roy their own personalities. This is most likely due to Rick's status as a megagenius, implying highly intelligent individuals are able to remember their real life as they play.

Roy's biography is not predetermined, as he is a video game character and his story is entirely dependent on the player's decisions.

Roy Parsons (C-131)

However, when Morty played as Roy in the episode "Mortynight Run," Roy first appeared as a child who had a nightmare. His mother reassured him, and he went to school. While there, Roy looked out the window during class and saw other kids playing American football. This inspired him to take up the sport and later join the school team. At one of the matches, he caught the eye of a girl in the stands named Rosy. They fell in love immediately and later married, having an unnamed son. However, a few years later, they ran into financial problems. Rosy told Roy he needed to be more realistic and talk to her father about the carpet store. He later developed cancer, and Rosy supported him throughout his treatment. After his recovery, she brought her wheelchair-bound husband home, where she and other family members celebrated his recovery. One workday, one of his colleagues asked him to move a Persian carpet. Roy climbed the ladder, but suddenly fell and died.

In the "Rick and Morty Presents: Mr. Meeseeks" comic, one of the Mr. Meeseekses decides to play as him. He wakes up and goes to help his mom make chili. A few years later, he began playing American football at the school. He later had a falling out with his father over football. There he met Rosy. They began spending time together and later began dating. She later accused Roy of cheating on her, and they broke up. He later dropped out of school and became effectively unemployed. He began to spend his time in bars, drinking heavily, out of grief. However, Roy found the strength to overcome his alcohol addiction and remained sober for at least a year. He unexpectedly ran into Rosy, and they decided to eat together at a restaurant. She was surprised he didn't order chili; he said people change. Rosy told him she thought love was her destiny. However, she later realized that love grows when two caring people share connection and understanding. Suddenly, a ventilation shaft fell from the ceiling onto her, and she died. Mr. Meeseeks, playing as Roy, was surprised by her death but was glad that death was possible in this game. He killed Roy by stabbing him, after which he played the game many times, dying in different ways.

Roy Parsons (Parmesan Dimension)

In the episode "Rick: A Mort Well Lived", Rick plays as the character in order to save Morty and get him out of the game after he is trapped in the game when his mind becomes splintered into all of the NPC characters in the game.

name: Simon
description_source_text:
Simon is a guy who works for The Pentagon and appeared in the episode Get Schwifty.

The original Simon presumably became a Cronenberg in the Cronenberged dimension.

He was summoned to The Pentagon by The President in order to handle the Cromulons that had just appeared in the sky. He told the President how every broadcaster on the planet was beaming out information on string theory, world history, the human genome and more, but was cut short by General Nathan. When Nathan presented his idea, launching nuclear missiles at the giant head, Simon was the first to speak against it. When Rick revealed the Cromulon to waiting for a hit song, Simon suggested Vivaldi, which Rick shut down and called him "Frasier". He later told the President that the magnetic poles were about to reverse due to the presence of the giant head.

name: St. Gloopy Noops Nurse
description_source_text:
St. Equis Hospital is a horse hospital where Beth works. Davin worked there with Beth. Jerry was incredibly jealous and suspicious of Davin. In "Rick Potion #9" Davin made overt sexual advances to Beth when he suddenly caught the "Morty love bug" and shortly thereafter turned into a "Mantis-person" as a result of Rick's first attempt to cure the illness. Jerry then burst into the room and beat Mantis-Davin to death with a crowbar, saving Beth in the process.

St. Equis Hospital first appeared in the Pilot episode, where Beth and Davin were seen performing surgery on a horse with the aide of Tom, who was reading the horse's heart rate. Jerry came into the ER, interrupting them while they were working, to try and convince Beth to put Rick in a nursing home. Beth argued with Jerry while working on the horse, and that caused the horse to get close to dying a couple of times. Every time the horse began flat-lining, Tom would inform them about it, which eventually drove Beth mad. Later in the episode, Beth was seen in her office, arguing with Jerry again. Davin then came in and started to talk to Beth about how passionate it was that they saved that horse's life together.

 In the episode Rick Potion #9, Beth had to go to the hospital because there was a seven horse collision and so she had to work with Davin. When Jerry heard that Davin was going to be there, he became extremely jealous and drove over there. After the surgery, Davin took Beth into his office with her. When he did, he turned out the lights and lit some candles, attempting to seduce and have sex with her, revealing that he truly does have feelings for Beth and also proving Jerry right. However, before Davin could go deep into his love for Beth, the Love Potion and mantis DNA had spread through the air vents and infected him, causing him to start changing his sexual feelings and begin having lust for Morty. He then started to chase Beth around the room like a crazed rapist, only talking about Morty. Jerry then busted in and beat Davin to death with a crowbar. The events of this episode were then undone as Rick and Morty traveled to a different dimension, where Rick had found a cure for the Cronenberg virus, meaning that things must have happened a little differently, undoing all of these events.

In the episode A Rickle in Time, Beth nursed a deer back to health in another Animal Hospital, claiming that she is a certified horse heart surgeon. Although the real St. Equis Hospital was not seen or directly mentioned, Beth's employment and experience with the hospital had major significance in this episode.

Beth lost her job in The Rickshank Rickdemption after the Federation introduced medicine that made horses healthy forever, rendering hospitals like St. Equis redundant. As of Rickdependence Spray, through means unknown, the hospital is still open and Beth is back at her job.

name: Communication's Responsible Rick
description_source_text:
Richard "Rick" Sanchez of Dimension C-137, commonly referred to as Rick C-137, is one of the two titular main protagonists (alongside Morty Smith) of Rick and Morty.

He is a megagenius scientist whose alcoholism and reckless, nihilistic behavior are sources of concern for "his daughter's" family, as well as the safety of her son, Morty. At the start of the series, Rick had returned to the Smith Family's lives after being absent for an unspecified amount of time, with the reasons for his absence being shrouded in mystery.

Rick began his journey as a young inventor in Dimension C-137, where he lived with his wife Diane and daughter Beth. As Rick repeatedly failed to create interdimensional portal travel, Rick Prime, an alternate version of himself, visited Dimension C-137 and offered Rick the tools to make it possible. When Rick turned down the offer, Rick Prime, feeling insulted, responded by murdering Diane and Beth. This motivated a devastated Rick to finally invent working portal technology, becoming the only version of himself aside from Rick Prime to do so independently. He quickly utilized his new invention by going on a lifetime hunt for Rick Prime with the intent to avenge his family. At an unknown point after this, Rick Prime punished Rick for his relentless pursuit by erasing Diane Sanchez from every single reality across the Multiverse.

Soon after beginning his search, Rick ended up joining the Resistance alongside Birdperson, and later, Squanchy, where they fought against the Galactic Federation. Following the Battle of Blood Ridge, Rick left to resume looking for Rick Prime and began killing countless amounts of his alternate selves in the process over the next few decades. Eventually, the other Ricks surrendered to Rick C-137, who formed the Council of Ricks and oversaw the construction of the Citadel of Ricks, although he left before it was completed. Rick, now an elderly man, landed in Rick Prime's original dimension, taking his place as the father of that dimension's Beth Smith, hoping one day Rick Prime would return. Whilst living in the Prime Dimension, Rick formed a bond with Morty, his new grandson, and began taking him on various misadventures.

Rick would finally get his revenge when he killed Rick Prime via an unlikely alliance with Evil Morty. With his main goal in life now complete, Rick initially fell back into his alcoholic and depressive ways until Morty helped him find a new purpose in their classic adventures. Rick was also able to move on from the loss of Diane by erasing his memories of her and allowing sentient memories of her and a younger version of himself live together in harmony.

As the series has progressed, Rick relocated yet again to another dimension twice, and has continuously committed more heinous acts that often leave many individuals dead and cause conflict with the Smith family. Rick's morality has frequently come into question, and many of the episodes shift him into a more antagonistic role.

While Rick's backstory remained shrouded in mystery for much of the series, it is clear that he always had a knack for science and invention, being able to rewire and fix a television around the age of 12. It wasn't until "Rickmurai Jack" where Rick's past is finally shown in great detail. The ’totally fabricated’ backstory shown in "The Rickshank Rickdemption" was mostly based on the truth and further hinted at in "Rickternal Friendshine of the Spotless Mort."

Prior to the beginning of the series, Rick had a fairly normal middle-class childhood and had developed an interest in tinkering with things at a young age. As an adult, he married a woman named Diane and they had a daughter named Beth. Rick eventually became a scientist who was struggling to invent teleportation. One day, he was visited by an alternate version of himself, who offered him the ability to travel to different realities, but Rick refused. Insulted by what he perceived as a lesser version of himself acting as though he was better, Rick Prime sent a bomb through a portal to kill Rick C-137's Diane and Beth. Rick grieved for an unknown period of time, before inventing his own portal gun and going on a quest for revenge. As more and more Ricks began hunting Prime for the same reason, Prime invented the Omega Device, throwing in a version of Diane to wipe out their wife across existence.

Rick would spend the next few decades searching for the Rick who murdered his family. He eventually became a weapons dealer, trying to find parts for a device to help him track down the one who killed his family. Rick eventually met Birdperson at a festival , and formed a rock band called "The Flesh Curtains" together with Squanchy. The three eventually formed the core of the resistance against the Galactic Federation, culminating in the Battle of Blood Ridge. After winning the battle, Rick offered Birdperson the chance to join him in his adventures throughout the multiverse, but Birdperson refused. Hurt by this, Ri

name: Morphizer-XE Customer Support
description_source_text:
The Tiny Customer Serive Reps were three tiny yellow men who worked as customer support inside of a Morphizer-Xe unit owned by Rick Sanchez.

The three customer service reps were included inside the Morphizer-XE unit ordered by Rick Sanchez at some point. They lived their lives inside the machine and most likely kept in contact with the producers of the Morphizer-products to report bugs and incidents.

Summer Smith found the Morphizer and decided to use it in order to enlarge her chest; the size of which she believed to be the reason why Ethan broke up with her to date Tricia Lange. After this failed, and Beth Smith was unable to properly work the machine, the tiny customer reps were called for help. They directed Beth to open a compartment on the side of the machine, which she did and thereby released the tiny creatures.

The three guys decided to retire and enjoy life on Earth, and no longer discuss anything work related. This did not last long before one of them was grabbed by a bird and carried off to be eaten.

description_source_text: "Rest and Ricklaxation" is the sixth episode of the third season of Rick and Morty. It is the 27th episode of the series overall. It premiered on August 27, 2017. It was written by Tom Kauffman and directed by Anthony Chun. The episode is rated TV-14-DLV.

name: Bartender Morty
description_source_text:
Bartender Morty is an alternative version of Morty who works as a bartender at the The Creepy Morty, a club located in the Mortytown district of the Citadel of Ricks.

Bartender Morty worked as bartender at the club The Creepy Morty during the time around the Morty Mart robbery. One of the nights he worked, Cop Rick and Cop Morty entered The Creepy Morty to meet with Big Morty. At the time, Bartender Morty was talking with Marty Morty and didn't notice or mind the two cops.

When the gunfight broke out, Bartender Morty hid behind his bar counter throughout the chaos. He did not emerge before Cop Rick executed Cop Morty.

description_source_text:
Cowboy Dancer Morty was a dancer working at The Creepy Morty who witnessed the fight between Cop Rick and Morty, and Big Morty. He is sometimes confused with Western Morty

After the near destruction, and later rebuilding, of the The Citadel; this Morty worked at the sleazy strip club "The Creepy Morty", located in the Mortytown district. He was paid to dance up on the central stage while dressed in some cowboy gear, to the enjoyment of drunk Morty customers. Cop Rick and Cop Morty visited the Creepy Morty during one of 's shifts, though he did not notice them. Only once the shootout started between the two cops and Big Morty, did stop performing. He assured them that his guns were fake and dropped them quickly. When the fight was over he promised not to say anything and crept away from the two cops.

name: Hairdresser Rick
description_source_text:
was a Rick working as a hairdresser at his own salon, "Rick Salon", at The Citadel. He worked to create a creature by mixing Rick and Morty DNA, but was killed by Rick Sanchez before he could finish it.

 opened a hair salon at the Citadel in order to collect hair from Ricks and Mortys for his experiments. He also employed a Morty, Bald Morty, to work for him and help collect customers' hair. One day they were visited by a pair of Rick and Morty, whom they serviced like usual until the Morty's phone accidentally dropped out of his pocket. Bald Morty secretly grabbed the phone and disappeared into the backroom, which the Morty customer mentioned to . He walked into the backroom and caught Bald Morty playing with the stolen phone and naming their secret experiment "Barbara Streisand". Tired of Bald Morty disobeying him, allowed the experiment to pull Bald Morty into its tank and dissolve the Morty into itself.

At some point later, was arrested for illegally cutting a Morty's hair without his permission. He argued in court that Morty hair was necessary for his experiments, at which point the accusing Rick realized what needed the hair for. threw his scissors in the Rick's face and escaped through a portal back to his backroom, where his experiment still resided. He was followed by the Morty he had attempted to shave and revealed what his experiment was, but was cut short when the Rick from the court room reemerged through a portal above him with a huge pair of scissors to impale . He died on the spot and bled out on the floor while the Rick and Morty released the experiment into the ocean.

name: Journalist Rick
description_source_text:
The reporter at Neeeews was one of the many fans of Mr. Poopybutthole.

When Mr. Poopybutthole got famous, all Sausage Fellas became obsessive fans of him, and were devastated after he disappeared without warning.

One day, a reporter at Neeews was talking about how there is still no sign of Mr. Poopybutthole, until he suddenly appeared with Summer. He apologized for his departure, but revealed that he's gonna remain on Earth as he has a better life there. The reporter refused to believe he would be capable of such a thing, so she accused Summer of brainwashing him. Everyone believed it and took Mr. Poopybutthole to Cecelia's studio, and Summer to Space Jail.

name: Private Sector Rick
description_source_text:
Private Sector Rick was a presidential candidate in the Citadel Election in "The Ricklantis Mixup".

Private Sector Rick was one of several Ricks candidates in the election for the next president of the Citadel of Ricks. He was initially leading the polls (until Candidate Morty took the lead following the debate and eventually won).

It was shown that he didn't take Candidate Morty very seriously and neither did the other candidates.

He can be seen in a side room, along with Rick Guilt Rick and Retired General Rick, when the bodies of Ricks killed by Evil Morty's order are being loaded into the airlocks to be disposed into space. Shortly afterward, Private Sector Rick was also killed on Evil Morty's orders, his lifeless corpse visibly floating in space with other Ricks and Mortys ejected from the Citadel.

name: Retired General Rick
description_source_text:
Retired General Rick, also known as General Rick , was an alternate version of Rick and a presidential candidate in the Citadel Election in "The Ricklantis Mixup". He used to be part of the Citadel's militia.

Retired General Rick was one of several Ricks candidates in the election for the next president of the Citadel of Ricks. He was the clear frontrunner of the election, leading the polls until the debate, where Candidate Morty took the lead and eventually won. (Although according to the guards, it was almost close enough to trigger a recount, indicating RGR came in a close second)

It was shown that he didn't take Candidate Morty very seriously and neither did the other candidates.

He can be seen in a side room, along with Rick Guilt Rick, during the scene where the bodies of Ricks killed by Evil Morty's order are being loaded up in the airlocks to be disposed into space.

It's unknown if he was present and died during the destruction of the Citadel or left it and survived his likely demise.

name: Secret Service Rick
description_source_text:
Steve is a member of The President's Secret Service. He appeared in "The Rickchurian Mortydate".

In "The Rickchurian Mortydate", The President sent two of his secret service members, including Steve, to summon Rick and Morty. Steve and his co-worker traveled all the way from White House to Smith Residence, only to find out that Rick and Morty could teleport there in seconds, using the portal gun. Steve questioned why they went through that entire ordeal just to do that, and his co-worker cut him off, telling him that he knows just as little as he does, being merely an employee who works for The President.

name: Steve Jobs Rick
description_source_text:
Steve Jobs Rick was one of the Ricks from the Shadow Council of Ricks, who was based on Steve Jobs.

Steve Jobs Rick is a Rick who is a member of the Shadow Council of Ricks. Whilst it is unknown what his exact role was, Steve Jobs Rick was running the Citadel alongside his fellow council members in secret.

After Evil Morty seized power, he asked the Shadow Council of Ricks to raise their hand if they agreed with Garment District Rick on his intent to keep the council in power over the actual president of The Citadel. Evil Morty then executed all of the Ricks who raised their hands. Steve Jobs Rick, however, was one of two Ricks who did not raise his hand, sparing him from the same fate. He then quickly sucked up to Evil Morty by complimenting his haircut to ensure his own survival.

What happened to Steve Jobs Rick after this is unknown. He could've been killed during Evil Morty's purge of any opposition following his rise to power or later on when The Citadel was destroyed.

was one of the Ricks from the Shadow Council of Ricks, who appeared to resemble an Arabic leader.

Sheikh Rick is a Rick that is a member of the the Shadow Council of Ricks. He was executed by Evil Morty, when Evil Morty asked who agreed that he had no control. Sheikh Rick was ejected into space with Campaign manager Morty, Rick Sanchez III, Cop Morty, Garment District Rick, and Investigator Rick. This was Sheikh Rick’s only appearance in the entirety of the show, so we might not see him ever again.

Sheikh Rick was part of the shadow council that he may have been friends with many of the other council members.

description_source_text:
Visor Rick was a version of Rick who briefly appeared in the episode The Rickshank Rickdemption. When Morty and Summer was brought to the Citadel of Ricks for their trial before the Council of Ricks, Visor Rick is briefly seen in the background with a regular Rick and Morty having a conversation with him.

In the same episode, many Ricks are killed when the Citadel of Ricks is transported into a prison of the Galactic Federation, it is unknown if Visor Rick is one of the Ricks who died.

name: P-Coat Rick
description_source_text:
Jerry Smith, of the Grand Central Dimension, also known as Boss Jerry, is the owner of Grand Central, a stopping point within the multiverse where Nomad Jerrys could pass through for a price. He served as the main antagonist in "Nomortland".

Boss Jerry lived in a version of the Smith Residence that notably lacked any of the other Smith Family members. It is implied Boss Jerry's family went on an adventure and never came back, leaving him alone. He formed a gang of Jerrys whose families hadn't returned from their adventures and subsequently turned the house into Grand Central, an interdimensional station where Jerrys travelling the multiverse could travel through certain routes by paying Boss Jerry. He also kept a druggie version of Rick as his slave and began wearing a leather jacket, which according to this Rick, turned Boss Jerry into a much meaner person.

Boss Jerry is first introduced when Mooch Jerry takes Jerry Smith of Dimension 5126 to Grand Central so they can request to get on the "Tundra Line" in order to get Jerry 5126 home. After it is revealed Boss Jerry employs Jerrys to steal from their multiversal counterparts in order to pay him. The meeting quickly goes sour and Boss Jerry blacklists Mooch Jerry and Jerry 5126 from travelling through the cracks in the multiverse. However, the two are able to get back to Grand Central and a chase ensues. Boss Jerry pursues the duo with his Bandana Jerry guards through multiple dimensions. It eventually ends with Boss Jerry trying to kill Jerry 5126 as the two climb through a burlap sack portal, where Mooch Jerry cuts the line, causing the portal to collapse in on itself. Boss Jerry is last seen falling through a void, presumably the space between universes, which seems to kill him or sent to another dimension.

Boss Jerry was a far more powerful and threatening Jerry compared to most of his multiversal counterparts. Before his singular appearance, Boss Jerry had decided he did not need to rely on Rick Sanchez the way other Jerrys do; Ricks need Jerrys to rely on. This is demonstrated by his enslavement and general treatment of Druggie Rick, who was frequently physically abused by Boss Jerry and made to follow his orders. Despite this, Boss Jerry is still shown treating his fellow Jerrys with respect if they pay him the demanded fare for using Grand Central, although relations can quickly sour if they step out of line. Boss Jerry is also not afraid of killing if need be, as he attempted to shoot Jerry 5126 and Mooch Jerry when they attempted to escape him.

name: Chang
description_source_text:
Chang was a NASA employee sent to pilot an interstellar vehicle, which turned out to be a trick by Rick Sanchez to save himself and Morty Smith from The Menagerie.

After Rick Sanchez and Morty Smith got stuck in the Collector's menagerie, Rick sent a message to NASA with instructions to build an interstellar vehicle to find them. Chang and Jodie was sent to pilot the vehicle, and met Rick and Morty in the menagerie. Chang and Jodie were tricked by Rick and Morty to trade places, allowing the duo to escape in the vehicle and leave the two NASA employees with the Collector. Rick later removed this incident from Morty's memories.

description_source_text: The Varrix are a species of light-skinned Humanoid aliens. They infest planets by disguising themselves as a planet's native species and nesting in caves. Their nests are spread across 300 galaxies, and they are the primary hunting target of Krootabulans.

Crystal Poachers came to be with the high value of Death Crystals on the black market. They travelled to Forbodulon Prime and mined as many crystals as they could carry, while armed with guns to attack other crystal miners. The poachers attacked Rick and Morty Smith once they discovered the duo, but Rick killed them all with ease by using a death crystal to predict when they were reloading their guns.

Crystal Poachers are poachers who illegally mine Death Crystals on Forbodulon Prime. A trio of crystal poachers were killed by Rick Sanchez while he was illegally mining his own Death Crystals.

Crystal Poachers came to be with the high value of Death Crystals on the black market. They travelled to Forbodulon Prime and mined as many crystals as they could carry, while armed with guns to attack other crystal miners. The poachers attacked Rick and Morty Smith once they discovered the duo, but Rick killed them all with ease by using a death crystal to predict when they were reloading their guns.

name: Hologram Rick
description_source_text:
Hologram Rick was a program Rick installed into Morty's spine in case he died to give Morty instructions on how to revive his Rick. He was later killed by Wasp Rick after becoming a physical being. He serves as the final antagonist of the episode Edge of Tomorty: Rick Die Rickpeat.

Hologram Rick was a AI program created by Rick and installed into Morty's spine to guide Morty into cloning Rick if he ever were to die, which happened on Forbodulon Prime. Hologram Rick was unable to convince Morty, who would rather follow a Death Crystal. Rick self replicated and started protesting Morty's unwillingness to revive Rick, but stopped when Morty threatened to flush away Rick's DNA. He questioned Morty on what death he was intent on pursuing, but quickly understood it to involve Jessica. He later returned while eating a bucket of hologram chicken and was offended when Morty asked why he pretended to eat chicken, sparking an argument on nutrition and food.

When Rick and Wasp Rick entered Rick's garage and found several weapons missing, Rick summoned Hologram Rick and accused him of growing sentience and trying to take over. Hologram Rick immediately called the accusation AI racism, before leading to the two wasps to Morty in the desert. They cut off Morty's connection to the ferrofluid, but it then latched onto Hologram Rick and turned him into a huge physical being. Happy to finally have mass, Rick proclaimed himself a god and stated they were through. Morty questioned this change and he explained that he was dissatisfied being a hologram. He attacked Morty and the two other Ricks. However, Wasp Rick managed to get in a sting on his left eye and laid eggs inside. The eggs immediately hatched into larvae that ate the insides of Rick's head and cracked open his face to fly out as wasps.

Hologram Rick was very aware of his status as a hologram and felt that calling the physical Rick the "real Rick" was holophobic. He considered all physical beings "density privileged entities", and took most of Morty's actions to somehow be considered offensive to non-physical beings like himself. He also considered Rick to be an AI racist for thinking he gained sentience and tried to take over. This all later turned out to stem from his discontent as a hologram, which he admitted after becoming physical.

He was programmed to be more tolerant than regular Rick in order to guide Morty into reviving his Rick. He was also afraid of Wasp Ricks, even though he was a hologram and couldn't be harmed by them.

name: Fascist Rick
description_source_text:
Fascist Rick was the version of Rick inhabiting the Fascist Dystopian Universe. He was killed by his own Morty, who considered him inferior and too political.

Rick used to go on adventures with his Morty before he ventured into politics and helped the President by sterilizing China. Being a part of the elite on his Earth, Rick would stroll around the house while wearing a bathrobe and drinking martinis.

In the middle of the night, he was awaken by Rick C-137 waking up in one of clones and exiting his underground lab. The two had a short chat in the garage before Rick revealed this Earth to be controlled by a fascist dictatorship. Rick C-137's hestitation to this realization had Rick ready his laser gun, but the two got interrupted by the Morty of this universe. Rick was accused by Rick C-137 of being a clone, which he denied and Morty agreed that his grandfather is the real one. Rick was then shot in the chest by Morty, who considered him an inferior Rick and too political.

Rick seemed happy to help another Rick, so long as the Rick was in agreement with the fascist dystopian government currently in control of America. He was ready to kill Rick C-137 for hestitating on the question of whether or not he was down with fascist dystopians. This version of Rick was less prepared to be killed than Rick C-137, who keeps a wide range of backup plans in case of an unwanted death.

name: Fascist Morty
description_source_text:
Morty Smith of the Fascist Dystopian Universe (referred to as Fascist Morty) was an alternate version of Morty from one of the several fascist dimensions that Rick visited. He died of suffocation out in space with Rick Sanchez and Fascist Gearhead.

Morty walked in on his Rick and another version of Rick in the garage, calling the situation strange. His Rick was about to shoot the foreign Rick for being surprised to be on a fascist Earth. The foreign Rick pretended to be the fascist Rick and called the other Rick a socialist who wanted to give all of their martinis to a family of immigrants. The fascist Rick said it was obvious that the fake Rick was the wet, naked one on the floor. Morty agreed that it was obvious, but that it would not be obvious to the police, and shot his Rick in the chest.

Morty went on to explain his action by calling his Rick an inferior Rick who was too political, while he missed the old fun Rick and Morty adventures like they used to go on. Rick agreed to this and told Morty he needed to go to Forbodulon Prime, but Morty disagreed and pointed his gun at him while yelling that he will go where Morty wants him to go.

He kept Rick at gunpoint while they traveled through space, and even brought a scared Gearhead along for the ride. Morty kept telling Rick that they would just go on a fun, classic adventure, which Rick had trouble following due to Morty not specifying anything he wanted to do. Morty then mentioned that he liked Mr. Meeseeks, and was told to grab a Mr. Meeseeks Box out of the glove compartment, but Rick quickly pressed the button and commanded the Mr. Meeseeks to kill Morty. The fight was shortened by Morty shooting a hole in the spaceship's glass, sucking them all out into space and suffocating them to death.

This Morty was a lot more strict and forceful than most other Mortys. He had no problem killing his grandfather, having already deemed him an inferior version of Rick.

He also refused to take orders from a Rick and pointed a gun at him when he tried to say what they should do. But Morty still maintained a slight innocent wonder, missing his old adventures with his grandfather and still enjoying the use of Mr. Meeseeks.

name: Revolio Clockberg Jr.
description_source_text:
Revolio Clockberg, Jr., more commonly referred to as Gearhead, is a recurring character who first appeared in the episode "Ricksy Business". His character is a representative of "social gearheads", people who know a lot of technical information, in Gearhead's case, it's information about the Gear Wars and engineering. Despite his and Rick's shaky relationship, they're still able to maintain a friendship, with Gearhead being invited to multiple parties hosted by Rick.

Gearhead is first seen as a guest at Rick and Summer's party, explaining the Gear Wars to Rick, as Morty anxiously paces the house. To escape talking to Gearhead, Rick calls Morty over and uses him as a distraction. Gearhead then begins to tell Morty about the Gear Wars. Later in the episode, he is seen playing a musical instrument similar to a lute to an admiring crowd, one of the onlookers being Jessica.

He makes his second appearance in "Mortynight Run", where he helps fix Rick's Shipsaucer. But when he finds out that the "Fart" in Rick's custody is priceless, Gearhead betrays Rick, pulling a gun on him. He reveals his resentment toward Rick, and that the nickname "Gearhead" is tantamount to a racial slur. Rick kicks him in his "gearsticles", then removes them and switches them with the gears in his mouth.

Gearhead appears again at the end of "Vindicators 3: The Return of Worldender", as a guest at Rick's party for Noob-Noob. Rick apparently ended his feud with Gearhead after getting high with him. In the post-credits scene for the episode, Gearhead uses Morty's Vindicators jacket to pick up girls. When an actual monster appears, he ditches the jacket and flees. While trying to escape he trips, and falls to pieces after hitting the ground.

Gearhead briefly appears in "Rickternal Friendshine of the Spotless Mort" in a flashback inside Birdperson's mind, at the funeral of Gearhead's brother Geardude. Rick also reveals that the core reason why he and Birdperson hang out with Gearhead is because he's their fallen comrade's brother.

Gearhead makes another appearance in "How Poopy Got His Poop Back", when Rick gathers some of his friends for an intervention for Mr. Poopybutthole, with Gearhead being the first one to be recruited. After gathering the group fully, they head to a bar called F#%K YOU'S, where they begin drinking and partying. Afterwards they hit several other locations, eventually meeting up with Hugh Jackman (who turns out to be Gene's brother-in-law) and moving their party to his house. After deciding that the crew is going to find Mr. Poopybutthole's ex-wife Amy, Gearhead and the others proceed to her house, where they discover she's having an affair with Predator P.I., who begins hunting them. During a confrontation with the Predator, Gearhead tries to talk his way out of the situation, but gets his head torn off and his "gearsticles" shoved in his mouth again, and wonders aloud why this keeps happening to him. After Mr. Poopybutthole's situation is resolved, Gearhead takes a "fading pill" and goes back to the Gear System, where he gets a spinal replacement.

name: Fascist Shrimp Morty
description_source_text:
Shrimp Rick is a version of Rick Sanchez that exists in a shrimp world.

When Rick first found Rick C-137, he came to help him and waved off the usage of his one of Operation Phoenix clones, noting that he had dozens of backup clones. After the two scared away Shrimp Morty, they discussed the unlikelihood of shrimp and primate dwellings being the same before Rick C-137 voiced his relief that Rick was a shrimp instead of a fascist.

Awkwardly, Rick revealed himself to be a fascist, sending C-137 running. Rick ends up being chased by fascist Shrimps and ends up dead before reviving.

Shrimp Rick makes a brief cameo in the multiverse scene of Rickmurai Jack.

When he is first seen, he appears to be a kind man by helping Rick and asking if he was okay after his cloning experience. Shrimp Rick also started to joke with his counterpart on their crustacean bodies and being curious about his world. However, when he learned that Rick was not a fascist like him he turned on him.

Like the other Ricks, he often belittles his grandson by insulting him with his counterpart.

name: Fascist Teddy Bear Rick
description_source_text:
Teddy Rick is a fascist teddy bear-version of Rick Sanchez who also speaks German.

 lives in a universe populated by teddy bear-like creatures, where a fascist regime called "BR" is controlling America, of which he appears to be a veteran. Like many other versions of Rick, he launched his own "Operation Phoenix" in case of his untimely death. Rick (C-137)'s backup accidentally got uploaded to Teddy Rick's clone and was greeted by his teddy counterpart, who immediately asked him in German if he was fascist. Rick (C-137) immediately took his own life to be uploaded to another universe where fascism was not the norm.

name: Bully
description_source_text:
The Bullies are three students that bullied Morty at Harry Herpson High School. They appear in Edge of Tomorty: Rick Die Rickpeat, with one of them having previously appeared in the ending credits of Something Ricked This Way Comes.

The secondary ginger bully bullied a young child at a playground and was beaten up by Rick and Summer as a result of his actions.

When Morty was stopped by the lead bully that bullied him in the school hallway, the bully made numerous threats in regard to his nonchalant attitude, all of which were ignored due to Morty's overconfidence brought on by the death crystal in his possession. After touching the bully's face, he smashed his head into a locker then promised that Morty would die the following day. Both the bully and his friends were present when Morty arrived with weaponry he took from The Garage the next day. After briefly taunting Morty, the lead bully took a swing with a chain but missed and was carried up into the atmosphere in several seconds. The other bullies attacked Morty, but one was gummed and the other was sucked into a very small box, which Morty then picked up and stored.

In the episode "The Old Man and the Seat", the lead bully appears on a milk carton in Jerry's refrigerator and noted as missing.

In the episode "Amortycan Grickfitti", the brown haired bully is seen bullying and making fun of Bruce Chutback after it is revealed that he is wore the same pair of pants twice.

In the episode "Full Meta Jackrick", the lead bully appears chasing after Marvin the Cowardly Security Guard on a shopping cart near a supermarket.

name: Morty’s Lawyer
description_source_text:
Lawyer Morty is a Morty who poses as a lawyer at the Council of Ricks.

He is put on Morty and Summer's side for their quarrel against the Council of Ricks in The Rickshank Rickdemption, where he is shown to be less of a serious lawyer and only there because "he's fun". He apparently has a pog collection.

In the same episode, many Mortys are killed when the Citadel of Ricks is transported into a prison of the Galactic Federation, it is unknown if Lawyer Morty is one of the Mortys who died.

name: Judge
description_source_text:
The Judge was the public officer appointed to decide the case against Morty Smith. She ultimately found him innocent and commited suicide shortly after.

The Judge from Prime Dimension presumably became a cronenberg.

The Judge was in charge of the court case against Morty Smith, who had killed teenagers, police officers and army soldiers in an appearantly unprovoked attack. With the amount of evidence against Morty, she was ready to sentence him, but allowed him some final words. He ended up saying "I will always remember our time in Peru", which turned out to also have been the final words of the judge's deceased husband. He continued on to call her "little sparrow", which prompted the judge to find Morty innocent. She then ran crying out of the court room, saying "I'm coming for you, condor!", and later taking her own life.

The Judge was very strict and professional in her line of work, but had a deep sorrow over her late husband. They gave each other pet names, like "sparrow" and "condor", and went on a trip together to Peru. Her husband mentioned this trip on his death bed, which the judge would not forget. She started believing in ghosts when it appeared that Morty was speaking on behalf of her late husband, and decided to join him in death.

name: Public Opinion Judge
description_source_text:
Christian Slater (born August 18, 1969) is an American actor, voice actor, and producer. He made his film debut with a leading role in The Legend of Billie Jean (1985) and gained wider recognition for his breakthrough role as Jason "J.D." Dean, a sociopathic high school student, in the satire Heathers (1988). He has received critical acclaim for his title-role in the USA Network television series Mr. Robot (2015–2019), for which he earned the Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film in 2016, with additional nominations in 2017 and 2018.

Born in New York City to a theatrical family, Christian Slater made his television debut at the age of eight on the ABC soap opera One Life to Live. He attended the Dalton School, the Professional Children's School, and the Fiorello H. LaGuardia High School of Music & Art and Performing Arts. In the 1990s, Slater starred in a number of big-budget films, including Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves (1991), Interview with the Vampire (1994), Broken Arrow (1996), and Hard Rain (1998) as well as cult films like Pump Up the Volume (1990) and True Romance (1993). His other notable films include roles in 3000 Miles to Graceland (2001), Windtalkers (2002), Alone in the Dark (2005), Bobby (2006), Nymphomaniac (2013), King Cobra (2016), The Wife (2017), and The Public (2018). Combined with his film roles, Slater has also starred in television shows such as My Own Worst Enemy (2008), Breaking In (2011-2012) and Mind Games (2012).

In addition to his live-action roles, Slater has had an extensive voice-acting career, with roles in FernGully: The Last Rainforest (1992), The Ten Commandments (2007), Igor (2008), and Mune: Guardian of the Moon (2017). He narrated the second season of Prehistoric Planet (2002-2003) and the Discovery Channel documentary miniseries Dinosaur Planet (2003), and voiced the character Slater in Archer (2014-2016), and Ushari in The Lion Guard (2016-2019). He has also been the voice of Floyd Lawton / Deadshot in the DC Animated Movie Universe since 2017. In Rick and Morty, he voices Vance Maximus.
